entries.WORLD_INFO_ACTION_DESC: You have a series of executable actions. Pay attention to the effects, restrictions, regions, and time of actions.
entries.WORLD_INFO_AUCTION_DESC: Every uncertain period of time, there will be an auction organized by a mysterious person, perhaps selling good goods.
entries.WORLD_INFO_BATTLE_DESC: Survival of the fittest; realm suppression is huge, high-realm cultivators have an absolute advantage over low-realm ones. If the opponent dies, the winner can plunder the loser's belongings.
entries.WORLD_INFO_CULTIVATION_DESC: Cultivation accumulates power until the current rank reaches its bottleneck. Breakthroughs can succeed or fail; success advances the character to the next rank, while failure may carry a cost.
entries.WORLD_INFO_DEATH_DESC: HP dropping below 0 also causes death.
entries.WORLD_INFO_EQUIPMENT_AND_ELIXIR_DESC: Weapons, auxiliary equipment, and elixirs provide extra bonuses. High-grade equipment and suitable elixirs can greatly improve a cultivator's survival and combat ability.
entries.WORLD_INFO_INTRO_DESC: This is a cultivation world where many cultivators compete.
entries.WORLD_INFO_LIFESPAN_DESC: Every character has a lifespan limit. When lifespan is exhausted, death follows; realm breakthroughs usually extend lifespan, while failed breakthroughs may shorten it.
entries.WORLD_INFO_ORTHODOXY_DESC: Different orthodoxies have different cultivation methods. Taoists respire, Martial Artists practice Body Tempering, Buddhists meditate, and Confucians educate the people.
entries.WORLD_INFO_REALM_DESC: The world's underlying standard realms, from weak to strong, are Qi Refinement, Foundation Establishment, Core Formation, and Nascent Soul. Each realm has Early, Middle, and Late stages. Different traditions may use aliases, but rules are calculated by this standard sequence.
entries.WORLD_INFO_REGION_DESC: Different regions have different effects; doing the right thing in the right region yields twice the result with half the effort.
entries.WORLD_INFO_ROOT_DESC: Spirit roots are composed of five elements (Metal, Wood, Water, Fire, Earth), divided into Single, Dual, and Heavenly Spirit Roots. Five elements interaction: Metal overcomes Wood, Wood overcomes Earth, Earth overcomes Water, Water overcomes Fire, Fire overcomes Metal. Heavenly Spirit Root contains all five elements and has an extra bonus during breakthrough. Cultivation efficiency is highest in regions matching one's own spirit root attribute.
entries.WORLD_INFO_SECRET_REALM_DESC: Secret realms open every few years. Entering a secret realm may encounter danger, but may also obtain high-level artifacts and techniques.
entries.WORLD_INFO_SECT_DESC: A shelter for cultivators. Joining a sect allows learning unique techniques and gaining protection from fellow disciples; rogue cultivators are free but lack resources.
entries.WORLD_INFO_SHOPPING_DESC: In city regions, you can buy Qi Refinement-grade elixirs and weapons. Purchased elixirs are consumed immediately to strengthen the character; purchased weapons can switch the character to a more suitable weapon type.
entries.WORLD_INFO_SPIRIT_STONE_DESC: The common currency of the cultivation world. Can be used to buy artifacts and elixirs, obtained through gathering, trading, or plundering.
entries.WORLD_INFO_WORLD_QI_DESC: The world undergoes celestial changes (such as Qi Tides) every few years, affecting character abilities.
entries.WORLD_INFO_RANKING_DESC: The cultivation world features the Heaven, Earth, and Human Rankings, as well as the Sect Ranking. The three personal rankings are grouped by equivalent standard realm: Heaven records Nascent Soul-tier experts, Earth records Core Formation-tier experts, and Human records Foundation Establishment-tier experts. The Sect Ranking ranks sects by total power.
entries.WORLD_INFO_DYNASTY_DESC: Mortal dynasties rule the realm, maintaining worldly order and borders. Emperors live no longer than ordinary mortals, and when one dies, a new emperor succeeds to the throne.
entries.WORLD_INFO_CULTIVATION_ALIAS_DESC: The same cultivation ranks have different names in each tradition: Daoists and rogue cultivators use Qi Refinement, Foundation Establishment, Core Formation, and Nascent Soul; martial cultivators use Body Tempering, Tendon-Tempering, Martial Master, and Martial Saint; Confucian cultivators use Vital Qi Cultivation, Principle Illumination, Mandate Establishment, and Great Scholar; Buddhist cultivators use Novice Monk, Monk, Arhat, and Bodhisattva; yao cultivators use Spirit Awakening, Form Transformation, Demon Core, and Demon King. These names map to the same underlying realm sequence; they change narrative and interface wording, but not breakthrough, power, lifespan, item grade, or other rules.
